title: Canada’s Transfer Pricing Transformation Will Get an OECD Buffer
summary: Canada’s Transfer Pricing Transformation Will Get an OECD Buffer news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: Canada is reportedly undertaking a transfer pricing transformation, with its approach expected to be buffered or influenced by OECD guidelines. This indicates potential future shifts in Canadian transfer pricing policy and regulations, aligning with international standards.

title: South Africa and Nigeria Drive AI-Enabled Transfer Pricing Audits
summary: South Africa and Nigeria Drive AI-Enabled Transfer Pricing Audits news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: South Africa and Nigeria are actively implementing AI-enabled technologies to enhance their transfer pricing audit capabilities. This development signifies a growing trend in tax authorities leveraging advanced analytics for more efficient and targeted TP examinations.

title: Transfer Pricing Reporting 2026 | Meeting Summary - PwC
summary: Transfer Pricing Reporting 2026 | Meeting Summary PwC
ai_summary: PwC has published a meeting summary discussing transfer pricing reporting in 2026, indicating ongoing developments and future requirements in this area. This summary likely covers upcoming changes or considerations for multinational enterprises regarding their TP documentation and reporting obligations.

title: ITAT Deletes Management Fee TP Adjustment as Issue Was Already Decided in Earlier Years
summary: ITAT Mumbai deleted the transfer pricing adjustment on management fees after finding that identical issues in the assessee’s own earlier years had already been decided in its favour. The Tribunal followed the principle of consistency. The post ITAT Deletes Management Fee TP Adjustment as Issue Was Already Decided in Earlier Years appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a transfer pricing adjustment related to management fees, citing that identical issues for the assessee in prior years had been decided in their favor. The tribunal applied the principle of consistency, emphasizing the importance of judicial precedent in TP disputes.

title: No TP Adjustment Allowed if Foreign LLC Income Was Already Taxed in India: ITAT Delhi
summary: The ITAT held that transfer pricing adjustment was not justified where the foreign LLC’s income was already offered to tax in India by the assessee. The Tribunal deleted the TP addition, finding no profit shifting or tax erosion. The post No TP Adjustment Allowed if Foreign LLC Income Was Already Taxed in India: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled against a transfer pricing adjustment, stating it was unwarranted when a foreign LLC's income was already subjected to tax in India by the assessee. The tribunal concluded there was no evidence of profit shifting or tax erosion, justifying the deletion of the TP addition.

title: TP Adjustment Quashed as Benefit Test Cannot Justify NIL ALP After Service Receipt Is Established
summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that transfer pricing authorities cannot assign a NIL arm’s length price when the assessee has demonstrated actual receipt of intra-group services through supporting evidence. The post TP Adjustment Quashed as Benefit Test Cannot Justify NIL ALP After Service Receipt Is Established appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad quashed a transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that tax authorities cannot assign a NIL arm's length price for intra-group services when the assessee has provided evidence of actual service receipt. This decision clarifies that the 'benefit test' alone cannot justify a zero ALP once the provision of services is established.

title: ITAT Delhi Deletes Section 44BB Addition as Revenue Failed to Establish PE in India
summary: The Tribunal held that offshore supply receipts could not be taxed under Section 44BB where the Revenue failed to prove the existence of a Permanent Establishment in India. The addition of Rs. 99.50 crore was therefore deleted. The post ITAT Delhi Deletes Section 44BB Addition as Revenue Failed to Establish PE in India appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi deleted an addition of Rs. 99.50 crore under Section 44BB, ruling that offshore supply receipts could not be taxed without the Revenue establishing a Permanent Establishment in India. This decision emphasizes the burden of proof on tax authorities to demonstrate PE existence for taxation of foreign enterprises.

title: Section 54F Exemption Allowed as Demolition of New House Is Not a Transfer: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: The Tribunal held that a residential unit does not lose its residential nature merely because it is located within a building housing a nursing home. Proportionate exemption under Section 54F was therefore allowed. The post Section 54F Exemption Allowed as Demolition of New House Is Not a Transfer: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam ruled that a residential unit retains its nature despite being in a building with a nursing home, allowing a proportionate exemption under Section 54F. The decision clarified that demolition of a new house does not constitute a 'transfer' for capital gains purposes under this section.

title: Section 69 Addition Reduced as Assessee Produced Evidence of Agricultural Income & Investments
summary: ITAT Surat held that the entire investment amount could not be treated as unexplained when the assessee had furnished supporting records relating to agricultural income and other sources. The Tribunal restricted the addition to 5% of the disputed amount and granted substantial relief. The post Section 69 Addition Reduced as Assessee Produced Evidence of Agricultural Income & Investments appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Surat reduced an addition under Section 69, ruling that an investment could not be entirely unexplained given the assessee provided evidence of agricultural income and other sources. The tribunal limited the addition to 5% of the disputed amount, highlighting the importance of supporting documentation for income sources.

title: Demonetization Addition Reduced for Cash Deposits Supported by Recorded Sales
summary: ITAT Delhi held that cash deposits made during the demonetization period could not be fully treated as unexplained money when supported by sales records and books of account. However, as the assessee failed to satisfactorily explain the abnormal increase in cash sales before demonetization, the Tribunal sustained only a lump-sum addition of ₹10 lakh. The ruling emphasizes balanced evaluation of evidence in demonetization-related assessments. The post Demonetization Addition Reduced for Cash Deposits Supported by Recorded Sales appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that cash deposits during demonetization could not be fully deemed unexplained if supported by sales records, but sustained a ₹10 lakh addition due to unexplained abnormal cash sales prior to demonetization. This ruling underscores the necessity for taxpayers to provide robust evidence for all cash transactions during assessment.

title: Commission approves €100 million Austrian State aid scheme to support cleantech manufacturing capacity - The European Sting
summary: Commission approves €100 million Austrian State aid scheme to support cleantech manufacturing capacity The European Sting
ai_summary: The European Commission has approved a €100 million Austrian State aid scheme designed to support the development of cleantech manufacturing capacity within the country. This approval aims to foster industrial innovation and competitiveness in the green technology sector.

title: Italian Free-Guarantee Ruling Boosts Transfer Pricing Toolkit
summary: Italian Free-Guarantee Ruling Boosts Transfer Pricing Toolkit news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: An Italian ruling concerning 'free-guarantees' is set to enhance the existing transfer pricing toolkit, particularly for financial transactions. This decision from Italy will influence how intra-group guarantees are valued and assessed under arm's length principles.

title: Italian Free-Guarantee Ruling Boosts Transfer Pricing Toolkit
summary: Italian Free-Guarantee Ruling Boosts Transfer Pricing Toolkit news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: An Italian ruling concerning 'free-guarantees' is set to enhance the existing transfer pricing toolkit, particularly for financial transactions. This decision from Italy will influence how intra-group guarantees are valued and assessed under arm's length principles.

title: Rajasthan HC Quashes Penalty against ITO As Error of Judgment Is Not Misconduct
summary: The Court held that an Assessing Officer’s quasi-judicial decision cannot attract disciplinary action merely because another view was possible. Absence of mala fides or lack of integrity defeated the misconduct charge. The post Rajasthan HC Quashes Penalty against ITO As Error of Judgment Is Not Misconduct appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Rajasthan High Court quashed a penalty against an Income Tax Officer, asserting that an error of judgment does not constitute misconduct. The court clarified that an Assessing Officer's quasi-judicial decision should not attract disciplinary action merely because another view was possible, especially in the absence of mala fides.

title: ITAT Remands TP Adjustment Issue as RPT Filter Was Not Properly Verified
summary: The Hyderabad ITAT found contradictions in the TPO’s reasoning for excluding a comparable company due to alleged lack of RPT data. The Tribunal directed fresh verification and recomputation of the RPT filter before deciding whether the company should remain excluded. The post ITAT Remands TP Adjustment Issue as RPT Filter Was Not Properly Verified appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Hyderabad ITAT remanded a transfer pricing adjustment case, citing contradictions in the Transfer Pricing Officer's (TPO) justification for excluding a comparable company due to alleged lack of related party transaction (RPT) data. The Tribunal directed a fresh verification and recomputation of the RPT filter to ensure accurate comparability analysis.

title: Section 68 Dispute Remanded as Source of Unlisted Share Funds Remained Unverified
summary: The Tribunal held that although the assessee produced documentary evidence supporting the loan, questions regarding the lender’s financial capacity and source of funds required deeper examination. The matter was remanded to the Assessing Officer for fresh verification. The post Section 68 Dispute Remanded as Source of Unlisted Share Funds Remained Unverified appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal remanded a dispute under Section 68 concerning the source of unlisted share funds, despite the assessee providing documentary evidence for a loan. The matter was sent back to the Assessing Officer for fresh verification due to outstanding questions regarding the lender’s financial capacity and source of funds.

title: ITAT Allows Appeal as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Invalid for AY 2010-11
summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not refer the property valuation to the DVO under the unamended Section 55A. Since the reference itself was invalid, the addition based on the DVOs valuation could not survive. The post ITAT Allows Appeal as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Invalid for AY 2010-11 appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT allowed an appeal, ruling that a valuation reference to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) under the unamended Section 55A was invalid for Assessment Year 2010-11. Consequently, any addition based on the DVO's valuation could not be sustained.

title: ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Not Valid
summary: ITAT Surat held that the Assessing Officer could not refer the property valuation to the DVO where the assessee had adopted a higher value based on a registered valuer’s report. The LTCG addition was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Not Valid appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Surat deleted a long-term capital gains (LTCG) addition, ruling that the Assessing Officer could not refer property valuation to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) under Section 55A. This was specifically applicable when the assessee had already adopted a higher value based on a registered valuer’s report.

title: Capital Gains Relief Granted as Section 55A Amendment Was Prospective: ITAT Surat
summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer the valuation issue to the DVO for Assessment Year 2012-13. Since the statutory conditions were not met, the long-term capital gains addition was deleted. The post Capital Gains Relief Granted as Section 55A Amendment Was Prospective: ITAT Surat appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Surat granted capital gains relief, affirming that the amendment to Section 55A was prospective, not retrospective, for Assessment Year 2012-13. The Tribunal ruled that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer the valuation issue to the Departmental Valuation Officer since the statutory conditions were not met.

title: ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Invalid
summary: ITAT Surat held that the Assessing Officer could not refer the property valuation to the DVO where the assessee had adopted a higher value based on a registered valuer’s report. The LTCG addition based on the DVO report was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Invalid appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Surat deleted a long-term capital gains (LTCG) addition, determining that the Assessing Officer's reference to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) under Section 55A was invalid. This ruling stemmed from the taxpayer having already adopted a higher property valuation based on a registered valuer’s report.

title: ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Was Invalid for Pre-July 2012 Transaction
summary: The Tribunal held that a land sale completed before 01.07.2012 could not be subjected to a DVO reference under the amended Section 55A. Since the valuation reference was invalid, the long-term capital gains addition was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Was Invalid for Pre-July 2012 Transaction appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal deleted a long-term capital gains (LTCG) addition, ruling that a land sale completed before July 1, 2012, could not be subjected to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) reference under the amended Section 55A. Consequently, the valuation reference was deemed invalid and the addition was removed.

title: ITAT Allows Appeal as DVO Reference Was Invalid Under Pre-2012 Section 55A
summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer the property valuation to the DVO for Assessment Year 2012-13 under the unamended Section 55A. Since the valuation reference lacked legal authority, the addition to long-term capital gains could not be sustained. The post ITAT Allows Appeal as DVO Reference Was Invalid Under Pre-2012 Section 55A appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal allowed an appeal, ruling that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer property valuation to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) for Assessment Year 2012-13 under the unamended Section 55A. Due to the lack of legal authority for the valuation reference, the addition to long-term capital gains could not be sustained.

title: Assessee Gets Relief as Section 55A Amendment Was Prospective, Not Retrospective
summary: The Tribunal held that the amended Section 55A could not be applied to a land sale completed before 01.07.2012. Since the DVO reference was not valid under the law then in force, the capital gains addition based on that valuation could not stand. The post Assessee Gets Relief as Section 55A Amendment Was Prospective, Not Retrospective appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal granted relief to a taxpayer, confirming that the amended Section 55A on property valuation was prospective and not retrospective, therefore inapplicable to land sales before July 1, 2012. As a result, the Departmental Valuation Officer's reference was invalid under the prior law, and the capital gains addition was rejected.

title: Registered Valuer’s Report Prevails as DVO Reference Failed Legal Requirements
summary: The Tribunal held that the amendment to Section 55A applies prospectively and could not be used for land transactions completed before 01.07.2012. As the assessee had relied on a registered valuers higher valuation, the DVO reference was declared invalid and the appeal was allowed. The post Registered Valuer’s Report Prevails as DVO Reference Failed Legal Requirements appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Tribunal ruled that an amendment to Section 55A applies prospectively and could not be used for land transactions completed before July 1, 2012. Consequently, the Departmental Valuation Officer's reference was declared invalid, and the assessee's higher valuation by a registered valuer for capital gains purposes was upheld.

title: ITAT Allows Registered Valuer’s Valuation as Section 55A DVO Reference Was Invalid
summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer the matter to the DVO for a transaction completed before the 2012 amendment to Section 55A. Capital gains were directed to be recomputed using the registered valuers report. The post ITAT Allows Registered Valuer’s Valuation as Section 55A DVO Reference Was Invalid appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that an Assessing Officer could not refer a valuation matter to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) for transactions completed before the 2012 amendment to Section 55A. As a result, capital gains were directed to be recomputed based on the assessee's registered valuer's report.

title: ITAT Deletes Capital Gains Addition as Section 55A DVO Reference Was Invalid
summary: The Tribunal held that for AY 2011-12, the Assessing Officer could not refer property valuation to the DVO when the assessee relied on a registered valuers higher FMV. The capital gains computation based on the DVOs valuation was therefore set aside. The post ITAT Deletes Capital Gains Addition as Section 55A DVO Reference Was Invalid appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that for Assessment Year 2011-12, an Assessing Officer could not refer property valuation to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) when the assessee had already relied on a registered valuer's higher Fair Market Value. Consequently, the capital gains addition based on the DVO's valuation was deleted.

title: Double Taxation Claim not Allowed as Income Actually Belonged to Assessee
summary: The Bombay High Court held that an assessee cannot avoid tax merely because the same amount was taxed in another entity’s hands when the income actually belonged to the assessee. The Court upheld the addition of ₹1.71 crore arising from under-invoiced export transactions. The post Double Taxation Claim not Allowed as Income Actually Belonged to Assessee appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court denied a double taxation claim, upholding an income addition of ₹1.71 crore arising from under-invoiced export transactions. The court affirmed that income belongs to the assessee who actually earned it, a principle highly relevant to transfer pricing and profit attribution in cross-border dealings.

title: Landmark court cases shaping the transfer pricing of financial transactions - International Tax Review
summary: Landmark court cases shaping the transfer pricing of financial transactions International Tax Review
ai_summary: This International Tax Review article examines landmark court cases that have significantly influenced the transfer pricing of intra-group financial transactions. It analyzes how these rulings are shaping methodologies and risk allocation for areas like intercompany loans, guarantees, and cash pooling.

title: DVO Reference Held Invalid as Assessee’s Valuation Exceeded FMV: Bombay HC
summary: The Bombay High Court upheld the Tribunal’s view that a reference under Section 55A was invalid where the assessee’s valuation was higher than the fair market value. The appeal was dismissed as no question of law arose. The post DVO Reference Held Invalid as Assessee’s Valuation Exceeded FMV: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court upheld a Tribunal's decision that a reference under Section 55A to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) was invalid when the assessee’s valuation of a capital asset exceeded its fair market value. This ruling clarifies procedural aspects of property valuation for general income tax purposes in India.

title: AO Cannot Refer Property Valuation to DVO When Assessee’s Value Exceeds FMV: Bombay HC
summary: The Bombay High Court held that a DVO reference under Section 55A was invalid because the assessee’s declared value was higher than the fair market value. The ruling reiterates the scope of Section 55A as it existed before the 2012 amendment. The post AO Cannot Refer Property Valuation to DVO When Assessee’s Value Exceeds FMV: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that an Assessing Officer cannot refer property valuation to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) under Section 55A if the assessee’s declared value already exceeds the fair market value. This decision clarifies the scope of Section 55A as it existed before the 2012 amendment for general tax valuation in India.

title: No Double Taxation on Liability Write-Back: Delhi ITAT Deletes Section 41(1) Addition and Notional Interest Demand
summary: The Delhi ITAT held that liabilities already written back and offered to tax in later years cannot be taxed again under Section 41(1). The Tribunal ruled that such an addition would result in impermissible double taxation. The post No Double Taxation on Liability Write-Back: Delhi ITAT Deletes Section 41(1) Addition and Notional Interest Demand appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT ruled against double taxation on liability write-backs, holding that liabilities already written back and offered to tax in later years cannot be taxed again under Section 41(1). The Tribunal deleted additions made under Section 41(1) and quashed notional interest demands, focusing on general income tax principles regarding cessation of liabilities.

title: Changed Jobs Mid-Year? How to File ITR with Two Form 16s
summary: Employees changing jobs may face additional tax at filing because each employer deducts TDS independently. The demand usually reflects a TDS shortfall, not double taxation. The post Changed Jobs Mid-Year? How to File ITR with Two Form 16s appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This article provides guidance for employees in India who have changed jobs mid-year, explaining how to file their Income Tax Return (ITR) with two Form 16s. It clarifies that any additional tax demand typically arises from a Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) shortfall due to independent employer deductions, rather than actual double taxation for individuals.

title: For TP comparability, functional similarity and scale of operations were crucial; inclusion of low-turnover comparable was set aside
summary: Where ITAT directed inclusion of Cepha Imaging Pvt. Ltd. as a comparable solely on the basis that it satisfied the export turnover filter without examining its functional comparability with assessee, and further directed inclusion of CG Vak Software & Exports Ltd. The post For TP comparability, functional similarity and scale of operations were crucial; inclusion of low-turnover comparable was set aside appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) emphasized the critical importance of functional similarity and scale of operations for transfer pricing comparability analysis in India. The tribunal set aside the inclusion of a low-turnover comparable, such as Cepha Imaging Pvt. Ltd., that was based solely on an export turnover filter without adequate functional examination.

This ruling reinforces the importance of a consistent methodology in benchmarking intra-group transactions in India.

title: Pune ITAT Deletes ₹5.67 Crore TP Adjustment; TPO Cannot Cherry-Pick One Transaction While Accepting TNMM for Others
summary: Pune ITAT held that once TNMM is accepted for a taxpayer’s aggregated international transactions, the TPO cannot isolate a single transaction and apply a different method. The ruling deleted the transfer pricing adjustment and reinforced consistency in benchmarking. The post Pune ITAT Deletes ₹5.67 Crore TP Adjustment; TPO Cannot Cherry-Pick One Transaction While Accepting TNMM for Others appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Pune ITAT deleted a ₹5.67 Crore transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that the Tax Officer (TPO) cannot cherry-pick a single transaction for a different method after accepting TNMM for the taxpayer’s aggregated international transactions. This decision reinforces the principle of consistent benchmarking methodology in India.

title: Entire Sale Consideration Cannot Be Taxed as Capital Gain: Mumbai ITAT Orders Fresh Computation
summary: Mumbai ITAT ruled that where a capital asset was acquired before 01.04.2001, the claim for adopting fair market value as on that date must be examined on merits. The key takeaway is that statutory valuation rights cannot be rejected on technical grounds alone. The post Entire Sale Consideration Cannot Be Taxed as Capital Gain: Mumbai ITAT Orders Fresh Computation appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT ruled that the fair market value of capital assets acquired before April 1, 2001, must be considered for fresh capital gains computation. This decision ensures that statutory valuation rights are examined on merits, preventing rejection on technical grounds alone.

title: Section 80IB Deduction Cannot Be Reduced by Section 80HHC Deduction During Computation: ITAT Delhi
summary: The dispute concerned whether deduction under Section 80IB should be reduced by the amount already allowed under Section 80HHC. ITAT held that deductions can be computed independently, provided the aggregate deduction does not exceed eligible profits. The post Section 80IB Deduction Cannot Be Reduced by Section 80HHC Deduction During Computation: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that deductions under Section 80IB and Section 80HHC should be computed independently. The tribunal clarified that the aggregate deduction should not exceed eligible profits, preventing reduction of 80IB by amounts allowed under 80HHC.

title: ALP Cannot Be Determined at Nil Without Comparable Analysis: ITAT Ahmedabad
summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that the Transfer Pricing Officer cannot determine the arm’s length price of intra-group services at Nil merely based on assumptions regarding benefit or commercial necessity. The post ALP Cannot Be Determined at Nil Without Comparable Analysis: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad ruled that the arm's length price for intra-group services cannot be determined at nil without proper comparable analysis. This decision challenges the TPO's ability to arbitrarily disallow a price based on assumptions of benefit or commercial necessity.

summary: Stellantis Ruling Clarifies VAT and Transfer Pricing Interplay Bloomberg Law News
ai_summary: A recent Stellantis ruling clarifies the complex interplay between Value Added Tax (VAT) and Transfer Pricing principles. The decision provides guidance on how arm's length valuations in intercompany transactions can impact VAT liabilities and vice versa.

title: SA closer to advance pricing regime, but thresholds threaten access - Moonstone Information Refinery
summary: SA closer to advance pricing regime, but thresholds threaten access Moonstone Information Refinery
ai_summary: South Africa is progressing towards implementing an advance pricing arrangement (APA) regime, offering greater tax certainty for multinational enterprises. However, concerns have been raised that proposed thresholds for accessing the APA program could limit its effectiveness and accessibility for many taxpayers.

title: OECD Seeks Input On Revision To Transfer Pricing Guidelines - Law360
summary: OECD Seeks Input On Revision To Transfer Pricing Guidelines Law360
ai_summary: The OECD has initiated a public consultation to seek input on upcoming revisions to its Transfer Pricing Guidelines. This outreach aims to gather stakeholder feedback to ensure the guidelines remain relevant and effective in addressing evolving international tax challenges.

title: ITAT Orders De Novo Adjudication on Taxability of Concessional Sugar Distributed by Co-operative Sugar Factory
summary: Tribunal held that the differential amount between the levy price of sugar and the concessional price at which it was sold by a co-operative factory to its members and non-members constitutes an unallowable appropriation of profit to be added to business income, or a valid business practice could not be decided without examining the factual parameters mandated by the Supreme Court in Krishna Sahakari Karkhana Ltd. The post ITAT Orders De Novo Adjudication on Taxability of Concessional Sugar Distributed by Co-operative Sugar Factory appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has ordered a de novo adjudication concerning the taxability of concessional sugar distributed by a co-operative factory. The decision requires re-examining whether the differential amount between levy and concessional prices constitutes an unallowable appropriation of profit or a valid business practice.

title: Commission approves €500 million Spanish State aid for agricultural companies facing increased fertiliser prices - The European Sting
summary: Commission approves €500 million Spanish State aid for agricultural companies facing increased fertiliser prices The European Sting
ai_summary: The European Commission has approved a €500 million Spanish State aid package designated for agricultural companies. This measure aims to mitigate the financial strain on businesses in Spain that are currently facing significantly increased fertiliser prices.
